rationale: The citing paper does more than list this source as background: it summarizes Gielens et al. (2008) in its prior-research table and repeatedly uses its findings to motivate specific hypotheses (e.g., overlap/proximity effects, size/financial strength, prior Wal-Mart exposure). The cited study is also used in interpretation, where the authors state they confirm related firm-level findings and position their contribution as adding store/category-level reactions and sales effects. This indicates a meaningful intellectual link in framing and empirical positioning, not a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing study builds on Gielens et al. (2008) as a direct predecessor on incumbent outcomes from Wal-Mart entry, then extends that line by analyzing U.S. local store/category marketing-mix reactions and their sales consequences rather than only firm-level event-study effects.
